applied_optimizations.name: Coalesced memory access for dim=1 reduction
applied_optimizations.description: Changed from one-program-per-output to tile-based approach where each program handles BLOCK_COLS=256 contiguous elements along dim2, enabling coalesced memory reads. Grid: (dim0, cdiv(dim2, BLOCK_COLS))

applied_optimizations.name: 2D tile loading with BLOCK_ROWS x BLOCK_COLS
applied_optimizations.description: Load 2D tiles of shape [BLOCK_ROWS=16, BLOCK_COLS=256] per iteration instead of 1D tiles. Uses tl.max(block, axis=0) to reduce the BLOCK_ROWS dimension within each chunk, reducing loop iterations from 4096 to 256 (16x reduction). This allows Triton's vectorization to work on both dimensions.

attempted_but_failed.name: BLOCK_COL=256, UNROLL=16 with simple loop
attempted_but_failed.reason: Larger block size and manual unrolling hurt performance

attempted_but_failed.name: Parallel reduction with 3D grid
attempted_but_failed.reason: Triton doesn't support tl.program_id(2) for thread-level parallelism

attempted_but_failed.name: Thread-strided reduction
attempted_but_failed.reason: Incorrect correctness - no mechanism for inter-thread synchronization

top_bottlenecks: Memory bandwidth - loading 16x256=4096 floats per iteration
Reduction overhead from tl.max on 2D blocks

notes: Problem: Max reduction over dim=1 of shape (128, 4096, 4095)
Key insight 1: Memory coalescing is critical - access contiguous dim2 elements together
Key insight 2: 2D tiling reduces loop iterations dramatically (4096 -> 256)
Original naive approach: 16.8ms (one program per output, strided access)
Coalesced 1D tile: 4.32ms (128-wide tiles, contiguous access, sequential loop)
2D tile approach: 1.65ms (16x256 tiles, tl.max reduction per chunk)

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_4
attempted_but_failed.reason: Three consecutive trials (2, 3, 4) produced no output despite increasingly specific instructions. With only ~26 minutes remaining and a consistent pattern of agent stalls, further retries are unlikely
